narrativeAnchor: Lobster does not speak for the user. It gives a high-value sentence a container that can be seen, responded to, and safely followed.
productConsistencyRules: Every card must originate from a concrete source message.
Every card is first suggested privately to the current user and becomes public only after confirmation.
Every card solves one social coordination problem only.
Cards behave like QQ-native enhanced posts, not like an external chatbot.
Cards must remain lightweight, dismissible, and non-blocking to the main message flow.

visibilityStages.description: Only the current user sees the suggestion affordance near the triggering message.
After confirmation, the card appears in the public message flow as a system-assisted post.
Actions update card state deterministically without changing the original source message.

messageFlowBehavior: Card insertion must not reorder surrounding messages.
Card width should read as a post attached to the message, not a full-screen takeover.
Collapsed state is preferred by default for dense message flows.

privacyRules: Suggestion detection uses the current source message and minimal local context only.
No cross-conversation profiling is displayed to the user.
No auto-posting into the public chat without confirmation.
No payment, transaction, or account linking behavior is allowed.

cardTemplates.intent: Turn a fleeting team-up shout into a structured joinable post.

actions.label: Join
actions.effect: Increment filled slots and show current user as joined.

actions.label: Remind Me
actions.effect: Mark the card as followed by the current user.

uxNotes: This is the best opening demo because QQ users can understand it instantly.
Keep the interaction focused on quick response rather than detailed game stats.
Show visible progress so the user feels the shout has become actionable.

cardTemplates.intent: Move potentially disruptive spoiler discussion into a safe side thread without killing the conversation.

actions.label: Enter Thread
actions.effect: Reveal or navigate to the spoiler-safe discussion layer.

actions.label: Hide Spoilers
actions.effect: Collapse the post for the current user.

uxNotes: This post should feel like a respectful diversion, not moderation punishment.
The main group sees an entry point, not the spoiler content itself.
This template carries the strongest PRD narrative about reshaping chat space.

cardTemplates.intent: Turn scattered merch interest into a visible trend without becoming a transaction tool.

actions.label: Interested
actions.effect: Increment interest count and mark current user as following.

actions.label: Notify Me
actions.effect: Mark the current user as a watcher.

actions.label: View Detail
actions.effect: Expand the post to show trend details and notes.

uxNotes: The post must never look like a built-in commerce or payment flow.
It should communicate trend visibility rather than transaction authority.
Use progress as a social signal, not as an order funnel.

good: Want to turn this into a team-up post?
I can move this into a spoiler thread.
Want to pin a tracker post for interest first?
bad: The system has inferred a merch purchase plan.
AI has automatically created a discussion thread.
